narrative: Structurally sound, well-architected engine with strong promises-vs-behavior alignment and clean customization surface. Downgraded from A due to a misplaced decision-log file, an unwired on_complete hook, missing headless/machine-readable output mode, and meaningful token-saving opportunities from unscripted deterministic checks.

broken.title: Decision-log file at skill root instead of references/
broken.detail: Path standards scanner flagged .decision-log.md at skill root. BMad conventions require all progressive-disclosure content except SKILL.md to live in references/.
broken.action: Move .decision-log.md to references/.decision-log.md and update any internal references.

broken.title: on_complete hook declared but not wired
broken.detail: customize.toml declares an on_complete hook (line 32) and SKILL.md references {workflow.on_complete} in the On Complete section, but no execution step actually invokes the hook. The field is a dead placeholder that will silently do nothing if teams override it.
broken.action: Either add an execution step in the On Complete section that runs the hook script (documenting the contract: available args/env), or remove the field from customize.toml to avoid confusion.

opportunities.name: Script-based FILE/CONTENT/METRIC evaluation
opportunities.title: Script-based FILE/CONTENT/METRIC evaluation
opportunities.description: FILE and CONTENT checks are deterministic (glob, regex, file reads) but currently performed by the LLM inline. Estimated 1,000-1,750 tokens saved per gate invocation; ~2.4-4.2M tokens/year at scale.
opportunities.detail: FILE and CONTENT checks are deterministic (glob, regex, file reads) but currently performed by the LLM inline. Estimated 1,000-1,750 tokens saved per gate invocation; ~2.4-4.2M tokens/year at scale.
opportunities.action: Implement a Python script (determinism-gate-eval.py) that parses checklist items, executes glob/regex matching, and returns compact JSON results. LLM consumes JSON summary instead of reading raw files for FILE/CONTENT items.

opportunities.name: Decision-Log Workspace for re-evaluation transparency
opportunities.title: Decision-Log Workspace for re-evaluation transparency
opportunities.description: Gate reports are regenerated on each run with no history. Gatekeepers cannot see which items improved or regressed between evaluations. No delta visibility for fix progress tracking.
opportunities.detail: Gate reports are regenerated on each run with no history. Gatekeepers cannot see which items improved or regressed between evaluations. No delta visibility for fix progress tracking.
opportunities.action: Implement Decision-Log Workspace pattern: append-only .decision-log.md alongside gate report. On re-evaluation, compare against prior report and highlight status changes per item.

opportunities.name: Headless mode with JSON output
opportunities.title: Headless mode with JSON output
opportunities.description: Other agents and CI pipelines cannot invoke gate programmatically. Report is always markdown with no structured return format. Automators must regex-parse markdown to extract gate status.
opportunities.detail: Other agents and CI pipelines cannot invoke gate programmatically. Report is always markdown with no structured return format. Automators must regex-parse markdown to extract gate status.
opportunities.action: Add Headless Mode section to SKILL.md with explicit parameters (phase, project_root, output_format) and JSON return schema. When output_format=json, return structured data; markdown report still written to disk.

opportunities.name: Artifact discovery for failed FILE checks
opportunities.title: Artifact discovery for failed FILE checks
opportunities.description: When artifact_pattern glob fails, report says 'no file found' with no remediation path. Users unfamiliar with HBC structure hit a dead-end. Non-standard project layouts cause silent failures.
opportunities.detail: When artifact_pattern glob fails, report says 'no file found' with no remediation path. Users unfamiliar with HBC structure hit a dead-end. Non-standard project layouts cause silent failures.
opportunities.action: On FILE check failure, perform a broader search (find project-root for similar names), suggest alternative paths, and link to the skill that creates the missing artifact (e.g., 'Missing D-19. Create with hbc-create-db-design').

opportunities.name: Explicit phase inference confirmation
opportunities.title: Explicit phase inference confirmation
opportunities.description: Phase inference from agent context is silent. If context is ambiguous (both BA and Dev roles), user may not realize which phase was evaluated. Can lead to accidental wrong-phase evaluation.
opportunities.detail: Phase inference from agent context is silent. If context is ambiguous (both BA and Dev roles), user may not realize which phase was evaluated. Can lead to accidental wrong-phase evaluation.
opportunities.action: When phase is inferred (not explicit argument), surface the inference: 'Inferred phase 2 (Design) from Architect context. Proceed?' Only skip confirmation when phase is an explicit parameter.

opportunities.name: Remediation skill links in checklist items
opportunities.title: Remediation skill links in checklist items
opportunities.description: Failed items list documents (D-02, D-19, etc.) but don't indicate which skill creates them. Users, especially first-timers, don't know how to fix failures.
opportunities.detail: Failed items list documents (D-02, D-19, etc.) but don't indicate which skill creates them. Users, especially first-timers, don't know how to fix failures.
opportunities.action: Add optional skill_to_create column to checklist markdown tables. On FILE failure, suggest: 'Missing D-19. Create it with hbc-create-db-design skill.'

opportunities.name: Enhanced evidence reporting for complex artifacts
opportunities.title: Enhanced evidence reporting for complex artifacts
opportunities.description: Evidence is free-form text (e.g., 'match count: 47'). For CONTENT checks on large files, reviewers cannot spot-check without re-reading source artifacts. QUALITY evidence lacks specific criticisms.
opportunities.detail: Evidence is free-form text (e.g., 'match count: 47'). For CONTENT checks on large files, reviewers cannot spot-check without re-reading source artifacts. QUALITY evidence lacks specific criticisms.
opportunities.action: For CONTENT: show top 3 matches with line numbers. For QUALITY: include specific findings (e.g., 'found 23 requirements but only 19 test cases; missing coverage for REQ-002, REQ-015, REQ-021').

opportunities.name: Parallel QUALITY review lenses
opportunities.title: Parallel QUALITY review lenses
opportunities.description: QUALITY items depend on single LLM evaluation. Single-reviewer bias can cause false positives or negatives on high-stakes gate decisions.
opportunities.detail: QUALITY items depend on single LLM evaluation. Single-reviewer bias can cause false positives or negatives on high-stakes gate decisions.
opportunities.action: For required QUALITY items, optionally fan out subagent review (skeptic + domain expert). Gate with a workflow.quality_parallel_review flag (default false). Consider when gates have >8 QUALITY items.

opportunities.name: gate_mode not exposed in customize.toml
opportunities.title: gate_mode not exposed in customize.toml
opportunities.description: gate_mode (strict/lenient) is sourced from _bmad/config.yaml, preventing per-skill or per-team overrides. Decision log justifies this as project-level policy, but rationale is not documented in customize.toml.
opportunities.detail: gate_mode (strict/lenient) is sourced from _bmad/config.yaml, preventing per-skill or per-team overrides. Decision log justifies this as project-level policy, but rationale is not documented in customize.toml.
opportunities.action: Add a comment in customize.toml explaining why gate_mode is excluded. If flexibility is needed later, add gate_mode as a scalar override.

opportunities.name: Phase checklist override discoverability
opportunities.title: Phase checklist override discoverability
opportunities.description: Phase skills can override checklists via {project-root}/_bmad/custom/hbc-phase-gate.toml, but this mechanism is only hinted at in SKILL.md comments. Users must hunt through docs to learn the override path.
opportunities.detail: Phase skills can override checklists via {project-root}/_bmad/custom/hbc-phase-gate.toml, but this mechanism is only hinted at in SKILL.md comments. Users must hunt through docs to learn the override path.
opportunities.action: Add a one-sentence comment in customize.toml field: 'Phase skills can override by setting the same scalar in their own _bmad/custom/hbc-phase-gate.toml.'

strengths.title: Scalable four-tier evaluation schema
strengths.detail: FILE, CONTENT, METRIC, and QUALITY evaluation types cover the full spectrum from deterministic to judgment-based. Each type has specific evidence requirements, preventing vague 'check it and decide' instructions.

strengths.title: Configuration-driven, not hardcoded
strengths.detail: Phase checklists, output paths, and completion hooks are resolved from customize.toml, allowing downstream phase skills to override without modifying SKILL.md. Gate mode and strictness are config variables.

strengths.title: Tight promises-vs-behavior alignment
strengths.detail: Every key claim in the Overview (strict reviewer, evidence-based, gate_mode controls) is enforced in the execution steps. No implicit-read traps or handwaving. Architecture analysis found zero gaps.

strengths.title: Compact, focused prompt
strengths.detail: SKILL.md is 82 lines / 1,158 tokens -- well within the single-purpose ceiling. No waste patterns, no pedagogical padding, no defensive commentary. Direct imperatives throughout.

strengths.title: Clean customization surface
strengths.detail: Six scalars and three arrays -- proportionate to scope. Clear field semantics, proper path resolution, merge-ready activation step arrays. No abuse patterns (boolean forests, identity fields, opaque names).

strengths.title: Correct intelligence placement design
strengths.detail: Decision log explicitly documents that FILE/CONTENT are deterministic candidates for scripting while QUALITY requires LLM judgment. The four-tier system maps perfectly to the 'deterministic where possible, LLM judgment where needed' principle.

recommendations.action: Move .decision-log.md to references/.decision-log.md to fix the path-standards violation
Wire or remove the on_complete hook -- either add execution logic in On Complete section or delete the dangling customize.toml field
Implement deterministic FILE/CONTENT/METRIC evaluation script to save ~1,000-1,750 tokens per gate and improve reliability
Add Decision-Log Workspace pattern for re-evaluation delta visibility and audit trail
Add Headless Mode section with JSON output schema for CI/pipeline and cross-skill integration
Enhance FILE check failures with fallback search and remediation skill suggestions
Surface inferred phase to user for confirmation when phase is not an explicit argument
Add skill_to_create column to checklist items linking failures to generating skills
Improve evidence detail for CONTENT (top 3 matches with line numbers) and QUALITY (specific criticisms)
Document gate_mode exclusion rationale and override mechanism in customize.toml comments

architecture.assessment: PASS. Solid structural integrity with proper frontmatter, conventions block, five-step activation pipeline, six-step evaluation flow, and minimal On Complete section. All asset references resolve correctly. The prepass false positive on '1-gate-checklist.md' was investigated and dismissed -- the actual files use phase-N naming and resolve via customize.toml variables. 82-line SKILL.md with 1,158 tokens is well within limits. No carve-out violations, no dead-ends, no circular dependencies.

determinism.assessment: FILE and CONTENT checks are deterministic operations (glob, regex) currently performed by LLM inline. A Python script could save 1,000-1,750 tokens per gate (~2.4-4.2M tokens/year at scale). METRIC checks add another 200-300 tokens. Implicit-read risk exists where LLM reads all artifacts upfront instead of only QUALITY-relevant ones. QUALITY checks correctly remain in-prompt. No scripts directory exists currently.

customization.assessment: Surface rated ABOUT RIGHT. Six scalars and three arrays are proportionate. on_complete hook is declared but unwired (the only real issue). gate_mode intentionally excluded from customize.toml (org-level policy). Phase checklist paths are well-parameterized but override mechanism is underdiscoverable. No abuse patterns detected. Persistent facts default is healthy.

enhancement.assessment: Primary gaps are UX and integration: no re-evaluation transparency (Decision-Log Workspace), no artifact discovery for failed FILE checks, no headless/JSON output for automation, silent phase inference. All findings are additive -- no architectural rework needed. Four user archetypes analyzed: Gatekeeper (needs delta visibility), Builder (needs discovery), Automator (needs structured output), Edge-Case User (needs fallback search).
